openingMessage: If you don't have time to do it right, when will you have time to do it over?
leadershipThought: We win in this business by being invited back the next day. That doesn't just come from doing the job right — it comes from how we carry ourselves as professionals and as people.
exxonMoments:
Good communication between all crews, continuous HS monitoring, and the job (Operational Discipline)
Good communication between all crews, continuous HS monitoring, and the job was

Roustabout crew member is fairly new (Operational Discipline)
Roustabout crew member is fairly new. Wasn't hitting a hammer union in the right

Incomplete OWL Submission — Blank Form (Operational Discipline)
A blank form submitted late in the day suggests end-of-shift fatigue is eroding the debrief habit — completing the debrief on-site before leaving is more reliable than submitting it after.

Incomplete OWL Submission — Blank Form (Operational Discipline)
Blank forms submitted late in the afternoon/evening suggest end-of-day fatigue is a factor in debrief quality — building the debrief into the job closeout on-site is more reliable than completing it later.

Incomplete OWL Submission — Blank Form (Two Technicians, Same Location) (Operational Discipline)
Two blank submissions from the same job at the same time is a crew-level debrief gap — the post-job review should be a shared step led by the senior person on site, not an individual task each person submits alone.

fieldNews.headline: Caleb Smith earned his Confined Space Entry & Rescue certification through Republic Safety LLC — Bobby Galindo posted the cert in the Breakroom for the whole team to see.
SM Energy's HSE rep walked the entire Schlitz A job while Dawson Drake was running it — two separators cleaned, LELs acceptable, and SM HSE left impressed. Client validation on the spot.
Edward Carrillo — MT on location at Nobels 2304, permit live, every HSMW crew member in the safety meeting before the task began. Sequence done right, no shortcuts.

Jacob Hernandez — Bizzel Irvin conversion: atmospheric testing confirmed safe, below-grade work with Dvir arm, Co Rep released the crew clean. He posted the full job rundown in Breakroom — that's ownership.
Kelby Hammer & Kyle checked into University 6-48E #105H and Josh Blassingame responded in Breakroom: 'The Dream Team right there!' — peer recognition, no prompting needed.
SM Energy Gipple 154H — 2,000ppm H2S, multi-week carry. Do not arrive without a confirmed monitoring and escalation plan.
fieldNews.detail: Dennis Easley — 2026-07-01 · elevated readings ongoing since prior week

fieldNews.headline: Cosmo phase 2 — H2S 3,000ppm / CO 3% confirmed at 9 AM atmospheric pull. Multi-gas hazard. Supplied air protocol must be confirmed before any crew re-enters this location.
Rattlesnake found under equipment on location — Ray Solis posted photos and flagged it. Summer heat is pushing snakes into shaded areas under trailers and equipment. Look before you reach.
fieldNews.detail: Ray Solis — 2026-06-29 · 'Be careful where you step'

todaysChallenge: On your next job, identify the line of fire before anything moves.
todaysChallengeSubtext: Where is the pressure release path? Where is the swing radius? Where should no one stand? Name it before work starts.
